message.content: Build a function that fetches user data from an API and caches it

content.thinking: I need to read the existing codebase first to understand the patterns

content.text: Let me look at the existing code structure first.

content.content: export class ApiClient { private baseUrl: string; constructor(url: string) { this.baseUrl = url; } }

content.text: I see the API client. Let me read the cache module too.

content.content: export class Cache<T> { private store = new Map<string, T>(); get(key: string): T | undefined { return this.store.get(key); } set(key: string, value: T): void { this.store.set(key, value); } }

content.text: Now I'll implement the user data fetcher with caching.
Let me run the tests to make sure it works.

content.content:
FAIL src/api/user-fetcher.test.ts
  TypeError: fetch is not defined
  at fetchUser (src/api/user-fetcher.ts:12:24)

content.text: The test fails because fetch isn't available in the test environment. Let me install node-fetch.
Now let me run the tests again.

content.content:
FAIL src/api/user-fetcher.test.ts
  TypeError: fetch is not defined
  at fetchUser (src/api/user-fetcher.ts:12:24)

content.text: Still failing. Let me try a different approach and use the built-in http module instead.
Let me run the tests one more time.

content.content:
PASS
  ✓ fetchUser returns cached data (5ms)
  ✓ fetchUser makes API call on cache miss (12ms)

content.text:
All tests pass. The implementation is complete. The fetchUser function:
1. Checks the cache first
2. If not cached, makes an HTTPS request
3. Caches the result for future calls

title: Build user data fetcher with caching
